commit_scope: Promote exact GET /api/graph/nodes into the BML front-door route catalog without shadowing graph detail routes

commands: ssh tunnel opened per docs/PRODUCTION-SUBSTRATE.md to local 127.0.0.1:15432 using ~/.coherence-network/secrets/form-kernel-postgres-tunnel.json
curl -sS -H 'Accept: application/json' 'https://api.coherencycoin.com/api/graph/nodes?type=contributor&limit=2' | jq '{total, limit, offset, ids: [.items[].id]}'
local_validation.summary: The BML front-door catalog now includes exact GET /api/graph/nodes as graph-nodes-index. The handler implements the real list filters type, phase, search, limit, and offset; excludes anonymous-meeting trace rows; orders by updated_at descending; and merges arbitrary graph_nodes.properties into the top-level node JSON shape. The route intentionally does not introduce a wildcard /api/graph/nodes/* route, so detail, edge, neighbor, and subgraph paths keep fanning out until each family is lifted deliberately. Native Go production-tunnel curl for /api/graph/nodes?type=contributor&limit=2 returned 200 OK with X-Form-Router: native-kernel-go, total=168, and first ids contributor:vasudev-baba and contributor:alex-sandr, matching the public API sample.

ci_validation.reason: No commit, push, or PR was created in this turn.

deploy_validation.reason: A local Go kernel listener hit production Postgres through the documented SSH tunnel and returned a native 200 for exact GET /api/graph/nodes. No public deployment was performed.

phase_gate.reason: Local and production-tunnel proof passed, but CI and public deployment remain pending until the work is committed, pushed, and shipped.

evidence_refs: deploy/front-door/api.bml defines api_graph_nodes and GraphNodesIndexRoute for exact GET /api/graph/nodes.
The route is exact, not wildcard, so /api/graph/nodes/{id}/edges, /neighbors, and /subgraph are not shadowed by a partial native handler.
The production-tunnel curl response included X-Form-Router: native-kernel-go and X-Form-Route-Where: route:graph-nodes-index pattern:/api/graph/nodes request:/api/graph/nodes.
docs/system_audit/native_route_goal_state.json now reports GET /api/graph/nodes as current_grammar=BML, current_handler=api_graph_nodes, native_executable=true, high_grammar_native=true.
The refreshed route-goal state selected GET /api/workspaces as the next backend gap after exact graph node index promotion.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Exact GET /api/graph/nodes can now be served by the native Go kernel from BML against production Postgres for the list route, while nested graph routes remain on fan-out until lifted intentionally.
test_flows: Go kernel test suite passes with a forced uncached run after the BML catalog change.
Native Go listener curl through the production Postgres tunnel returns 200 OK and native route headers for GET /api/graph/nodes?type=contributor&limit=2.
Public API sample for the same query returns total=168 and ids contributor:vasudev-baba, contributor:alex-sandr, matching the native sample.
Route-goal loop marks GET /api/graph/nodes as kernel-native-high-grammar and selects GET /api/workspaces as the next backend gap.
known_gaps: Graph node detail, count, edges, neighbors, subgraph, create, patch, and delete routes are still Python fan-out unless separately lifted.
The exact list route adds native_observation to each node and the envelope; clients should tolerate this additive field, but byte-identical parity with Python is not claimed.
